title: [BUG] Table Rows and Columns are undefined/ incomplete
author: Walnussbaer
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

When trying to read the columns and rows of an Excel data table, rows are undefined and columns are incomplete. 

Context: I am developing an Electron app. The exceljs processing is done in Node context (Backend of Electron App, not in browser context) 

Lib version: 4.4.0

## Steps To Reproduce

<!-- The exact steps required to reproduce the issue, ideally with a code example -->

```javascript
// in my case, a table with more than 3 columns should be loaded
const table = worksheet.getTable(tableName);
const columns = table.columns
// --> columns is undefined --> Bug

const columnsB = table.model.columns
// --> columnsB is an array of 3 columns, but my table has way more then 3 columns (ExcelJs only reads the first 3 columns) --> Bug

const rows = table.rows
// --> rows is undefined --> Bug
```

Some output from the debugger:
![grafik](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/49414693/47ad3e78-9a3e-44d6-a88a-0c5da29fbe60)

The actual table in Excel
![grafik](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/49414693/a1fb9dd8-ffa5-4a90-afc6-16b31d8fea1f)



## The expected behaviour:

I expect that all 3 variables mentioned above (columns, columnsB and rows) are not undefined/ contain the correct table data. 

EDIT: I tried reading a different table in the workbook. It is always the same result: table.model.columns always only contains the first 3 columns of the table, the rest is missing. 


## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

-

comments.author: henrymcl
comments.body:
It seems that both rows and columns are missing.
We can resort to reading the cells directly for the moment.
